mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring external triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.13% as of 2024-09-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 710773M and HOUST at 1356K as of 2024-08-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint signal produces negative Rank IC when tested independently against single-series alternatives.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces forward earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and margin expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.1389 (vintage) vs 100.4309 (revised) as of 2024-08-01 shows significant revision risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a 1-month window signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors flee growth-sensitive positions.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50K and UNRATE rises > 0.1pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159105K (vintage) vs 157912K (revised) shows high revision risk
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate below 2% signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity risk premiums and reduce forward earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2% in latest quarter
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sub-2% GDP growth are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 3% year-over-year, the combination signals restrictive monetary policy that constrains corporate borrowing costs and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to be met simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.13% and CPIAUCSL at 314.686 as of 2024-09-01
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% over a 2-month rolling average signals strengthening residential demand that indicates robust consumer confidence and future economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1356K (vintage) vs 1391K (revised) as of 2024-08-01 shows upward revision
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following strong housing starts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
